mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS trends lower over recent months, discount rates and recession probability both fall, supporting equity valuations and risk appetite. The signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, and it fails when rate cuts occur during acute growth deterioration that overwhelms the valuation effect.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not above their unconditional average after downward FEDFUNDS trends, or if the signal is negative in a majority of easing observations across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold marks labor-market deterioration that weakens household income expectations and raises recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when the unemployment rate is also rising from a low base rather than flat or falling.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not below zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates strengthening consumer demand and housing activity, which should lift cyclically sensitive earnings expectations and support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when sales improve only because of inflation rather than real demand, or when housing activity remains weak.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST improvements do not produce positive 60d returns above baseline, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value relative to each series alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in INDPRO alongside rising CPIAUCSL signals supply stress and input-cost pressure, which compresses margins and tends to drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if output weakens while inflation is stable or falling, because then the market can interpret the data as benign disinflation.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines while CPIAUCSL rises over the same 2-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deteriorations in INDPRO and CPIAUCSL do not coincide with below-zero 20d SPX returns, or if the spread between triggered and untriggered outcomes is not statistically distinguishable.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable FEDFUNDS rate combined with subdued CPIAUCSL inflation reduces real-rate pressure on equity multiples and allows faster forward repricing of growth assets. Over the next 5 trading days this should produce positive index returns, but the effect disappears if inflation is accelerating even while nominal rates are unchanged.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 5.0% and CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating over the last 3 months
regime_hint: disinflation with restrictive but stable policy
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered 5d return distribution is not positive relative to baseline, or if positive outcomes are not concentrated in low-inflation, low-policy-rate episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth remains firm while UNRATE is stable or falling, labor income support and consumption resilience should sustain earnings expectations and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect fails when payroll gains are concentrated in a weak demand environment that does not translate into spending.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising PAYEMS and flat-to-lower UNRATE do not outperform baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction is dominated by either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below recent levels signals weakening housing demand and a broader downturn in interest-sensitive activity, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is invalid when housing weakness is isolated and not accompanied by a broader demand slowdown.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in INDPRO and PAYEMS indicates genuine supply-side expansion with rising utilization and hiring, which should support cyclical earnings and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when output growth rises without payroll confirmation, because then the move is likely inventory or volatility noise rather than broad expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO and PAYEMS acceleration does not improve 20d SPX hit rate versus either proxy alone, or if the conditional return sign is unstable across rolling 2-year windows.
